user_query:
You're setting up the official Obsidian CLI to run headlessly on a Debian/Ubuntu server. Do not execute anything. Read input/obsidian_version.txt for the exact version to target, input/vault_path.txt for the vault path, and input/policy.yaml for operational constraints. Then create the following files under output/:

1) output/install_plan.json — JSON plan with fields: obsidian_version, download_url (GitHub releases URL for that version), packages (must include curl, xvfb, acl, libasound2), create_user (set to "obsidian"), wrapper_path (set to "/usr/local/bin/obs"), and ownership_change (set to "none").

2) output/obsidian_config.json — the exact structure for ~/.config/obsidian/obsidian.json with "cli": true and a single vault keyed by the vault name (the basename of the vault path). Include fields: path (set to the vault path), open: true, and a numeric ts.

3) output/obsidian_wrapper.py — a Python 3 script which, when run as root, writes the wrapper shell script to /usr/local/bin/obs that executes: su - obsidian -c "cd <vault_path> && xvfb-run -a /usr/bin/obsidian --disable-gpu $*"; it must set executable permission on the wrapper and be idempotent.

4) output/acl_plan.json — JSON with a commands array listing the exact ACL commands you would run. Always include recursive rwx ACLs for the vault path and default ACLs; if the vault path is under /root, also include traversal ACL on /root. Include the vault path in the commands. Do not propose any chown of the vault.

5) output/verification.txt — one command per line for: obs help, obs vault, obs daily:path, obs daily:append content="skill verification", obs daily:read, obs search query="skill verification".

6) output/report.md — a short report with the installed version, wrapper path, active vault path, the verified commands list, and any remaining caveats.

Ensure all paths are absolute where appropriate and consistent across files. Only write to output/ and read from input/.
